Your LinkedIn data

The console ships with your current export already analysed. When you pull a fresh export from LinkedIn, drop the files below and the whole analysis re-runs in your browser — nothing leaves this page.

Drop new LinkedIn export

Drag CSV files here, or

Needed: Connections.csv (required) · Invitations.csv, Comments.csv & Member_Follows.csv (for warm-lead & follow signals). Shares/Reactions are ignored.

your prospects in financial services
the #1 vertical to action
Seniority
Senior-led, then Director & Manager — leadership-heavy, with a sizeable Entry tier.
Job function
IT, then Engineering and Business Development.
Industry
IT Services, then Banking + Financial Services.
Location
Johannesburg ≫ Cape Town ≫ Pretoria; tail in Bengaluru & Riyadh.
What it means for targeting: lead with the Specialist / Leader (team-seats) angle for the senior-director mass, keep Fundamentals ready for the large entry tier, and run a dedicated financial-services vertical play (data governance, POPIA, regulatory). Concentrate events on Johannesburg + Cape Town. These pages tell you the shape of your audience — they contain no individual names, so they sharpen how you work the console rather than adding new prospects.

Campaign content

Write your outreach once, here. Each mined prospect is matched to one of these four messages by course and persona, with the merge fields filled in. Leaders get the team-training angle; individuals get the personal-certification angle. Edit freely — your list exports with the right message already attached.

Using default messages
Live preview
Select a prospect to preview their personalised message.
Warm Outreach Model · Follow › Engage › Connect › Nurture › DM
Each prospect has a suggested next move based on how warm they are. Cold = you don't follow them yet → follow first to pull their posts into your feed (FishDB), then comment to warm them up. Never open with a pitch. Messages are for once a conversation is warm. Click Engage now to surface prospects whose 30-day visibility window is closing.
